items.title: Tre Scott, Nets Sign Contract
items.blurb: Tre Scott, Nets Sign Contract — The Brooklyn Nets have signed forward Trevon Scott to a contract. Scott, 6-foot-8 and 225 pounds, originally joined Brooklyn on a 10-day hardship contract April 2. Scott averaged 8.0 points, 5.6 rebounds, 1.8 assists, and 1.4 steals in 31.2 minutes across five appearances. items.title: Clippers make NBA history with remarkable turnaround
items.blurb: Clippers make NBA history with remarkable turnaround — A team that started the season with big expectations and a veteran group of future Hall of Famers finishing as the No. 9 seed is disappointing. However, the Clippers even being in the play-in tournament is actually an historic turn around based on where they were early in the season.

items.title: James Harden Notched Historic Career Feat On Monday
items.blurb: James Harden Notched Historic Career Feat On Monday —   Los Angeles Clippers guard James Harden entered Monday’s matchup against the Philadelphia 76ers needing just one point to reach a significant career milestone. The Clippers fell to Philadelphia in a closely contested game at the Wells Fargo Center on Tuesday, but Harden delivered 28 points along with six rebounds and five assists.

items.title: Devin Booker Signs Two-Year, $145 Million Max Contract with Suns
items.blurb: Devin Booker Signs Two-Year, $145 Million Max Contract with Suns — On July 10, 2025, Devin Booker made NBA history by signing a two-year, $145 million max extension with the Phoenix Suns.
